Operating systems often log commands for the processes that they run, and you wouldn't want the log to include a secret that you passed in as an input. Use the script's environment or map the variable within the variables block to pass secrets to your pipeline. Incorrect Answers: B: Never pass secrets on the command line. C: Adding a prefix does not make the variable a secret. The issecret property makes it secret but does not prevent logging of the se...

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You plan to create a GitHub workflow that will use GitHub Actions. The actions will require a 256-KB secret.
questions.questionText: You plan to create a GitHub workflow that will use GitHub Actions. The actions will require a 256-KB secret. You need to recommend a solution to store and encrypt the secret. The secret value must be accessible only to the workflow. The solution must minimize administrative effort What should you recommend?
options: A. Store the secret in the organization-level GitHub secrets.
B. Store the secret in the repository-level GitHub secrets.
C. Encrypt the secret value and store the value in the repository. Store the decryption key in the repository-level GitHub secrets.
questions.explanation: GitHub secrets have a size limit of 64 KB. Since the secret is 256 KB, it exceeds this limit and cannot be stored directly in GitHub secrets (repository-level or organization-level). The recommended approach is to encrypt the secret value using a tool like gpg, store the encrypted file in the repository, and then store the decryption key (which is small) as a repository-level GitHub secret. The workflow can then decrypt the secret at runtime. Incorrect answers: A (organization-level secrets) is wrong because organization secrets have the same 64 KB size limit. B (repository-level secrets) is wrong because they also have the same 64 KB size limit and cannot store a 256 KB secret.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have a GitHub Enterprise account.
questions.questionText: You have a GitHub Enterprise account. You need to enable push protection for secret scanning of the account repositories. What should you do first?
options: A. Purchase a GitHub Advanced Security license.
questions.explanation: Push protection for secret scanning in GitHub Enterprise requires GitHub Advanced Security (GHAS). GHAS is a paid add-on that provides advanced security features including code scanning, secret scanning with push protection, and dependency review. Without GHAS, push protection for secret scanning is not available. Incorrect answers: B (Premium Plus support) is wrong because support tiers do not enable security features like push protection. C (Enforce MFA) is wrong because multi-factor authentication secures user accounts but does not enable push protection for secret scanning.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - Your company uses Azure DevOps and Microsoft Azure Active Directory (Azure AD), part of Microsoft Entra.
questions.questionText: Your company uses Azure DevOps and Microsoft Azure Active Directory (Azure AD), part of Microsoft Entra. Only users who have accounts in Azure AD can access the Azure DevOps environment. You need to ensure that only devices that are connected to the on-premises network can access the Azure DevOps environment. What should you do?
options: A. Assign the Stakeholder access level to all users.
B. In Azure DevOps, configure Security in Project Settings.
C. In Azure AD, configure conditional access.
questions.explanation: Azure AD Conditional Access policies allow you to control access based on conditions such as device location, device compliance, and network location. By configuring a Conditional Access policy that requires devices to be on the on-premises network, you can restrict Azure DevOps access to only those devices connected to the corporate network. Incorrect answers: A (Stakeholder access level) is wrong because access levels control feature permissions within Azure DevOps, not network-based access restrictions. B (Security in Project Settings) is wrong because project security settings control permissions within projects, not device or network-based access conditions.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have an Azure subscription that contains four Azure virtual machines.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains four Azure virtual machines. You need to configure the virtual machines to use a single identity. The solution must meet the following requirements: • Ensure that the credentials for the identity are managed automatically. • Support granting privileges to the identity. Which type of identity should you use?
questions.explanation: A user-assigned managed identity is the correct choice because it can be shared across multiple Azure virtual machines (all four VMs use the same identity), credentials are managed automatically by Azure (no password rotation needed), and you can grant privileges to it through Azure RBAC. Incorrect answers: A (system-assigned managed identity) is wrong because each system-assigned identity is tied to a single resource and cannot be shared across multiple VMs. C (service principal) is wrong because while a service principal can be shared, its credentials are not managed automatically - you must manually rotate secrets or certificates.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have a GitHub repository that contains multiple workflows and a secret stored at the environment level.
questions.questionText: You have a GitHub repository that contains multiple workflows and a secret stored at the environment level. You need to ensure that the secret can be used by all the workflows. What should you do first?
options: A. Recreate the secret at the organization level.
B. Recreate the secret at the repository level.
questions.explanation: Environment-level secrets are only accessible to workflows that reference that specific environment. To make a secret available to all workflows in a repository, you need to recreate it at the repository level. Repository-level secrets are accessible to all workflows within that repository. Incorrect answers: A (organization level) is wrong because while organization secrets are accessible across repositories, the requirement is to make it available only within the repository's workflows, and organization-level would be broader than needed.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You are designing the development process for your company.
questions.questionText: You are designing the development process for your company. You need to recommend a solution for continuous inspection of the company's code base to locate common code patterns that are known to be problematic. What should you include in the recommendation?
options: A. Microsoft Visual Studio test plans
questions.explanation: SonarCloud is a cloud service offered by SonarSource and based on SonarQube. SonarQube is a widely adopted open source platform to inspect continuously the quality of source code and detect bugs, vulnerabilities and code smells in more than 20 different languages. Note: The SonarCloud Azure DevOps extension brings everything you need to have your projects analyzed on SonarCloud very quickly. Incorrect Answers: A: Test plans are used to group together test suites and individual test cases. This i...

questions.topic: Topic 5 - Your company uses Azure DevOps for the build pipelines and deployment pipelines of Java-based projects.
questions.questionText: Your company uses Azure DevOps for the build pipelines and deployment pipelines of Java-based projects. You need to recommend a strategy for managing technical debt. Which two actions should you include in the recommendation? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
options: A. Configure post-deployment approvals in the deployment pipeline.
B. Configure pre-deployment approvals in the deployment pipeline.
C. Integrate Azure DevOps and SonarQube.
questions.explanation: B: With SonarQube pre-approval, you can set quality gate. C: You can manage technical debt with SonarQube and Azure DevOps. Note: Technical debt is the set of problems in a development effort that make forward progress on customer value ineffcient. Technical debt saps productivity by making code hard to understand, fragile, time-consuming to change, diffcult to validate, and creates unplanned work that blocks progress. Unless they are managed, technical debt can accumulate and hurt the overall qua...

questions.topic: Topic 5 - Your company is building a new solution in Java.
questions.questionText: Your company is building a new solution in Java. The company currently uses a SonarQube server to analyze the code of .NET solutions. You need to analyze and monitor the code quality of the Java solution. Which task types should you add to the build pipeline?
questions.explanation: SonarQube is a set of static analyzers that can be used to identify areas of improvement in your code. It allows you to analyze the technical debt in your project and keep track of it in the future. With Maven and Gradle build tasks, you can run SonarQube analysis with minimal setup in a new or existing Azure DevOps Services build task. Prepare Analysis Configuration task, to configure all the required settings before executing the build. ✑ This task is mandatory. ✑ In case of .NET solutions or Ja...

questions.topic: Topic 5 - Your company is building a new solution in Java.
questions.questionText: Your company is building a new solution in Java. The company currently uses a SonarQube server to analyze the code of .NET solutions. You need to analyze and monitor the code quality of the Java solution. Which task types should you add to the build pipeline?
questions.explanation: SonarQube is a set of static analyzers that can be used to identify areas of improvement in your code. It allows you to analyze the technical debt in your project and keep track of it in the future. With Maven and Gradle build tasks, you can run SonarQube analysis with minimal setup in a new or existing Azure DevOps Services build task. Prepare Analysis Configuration task, to configure all the required settings before executing the build. ✑ This task is mandatory. ✑ In case of .NET solutions or Ja...

questions.topic: Topic 5 - Your company uses Azure DevOps for the build pipelines and deployment pipelines of Java-based projects.
questions.questionText: Your company uses Azure DevOps for the build pipelines and deployment pipelines of Java-based projects. You need to recommend a strategy for managing technical debt. Which action should you include in the recommendation?
options: A. Configure post-deployment approvals in the deployment pipeline.
B. Integrate Azure DevOps and SonarQube.
questions.explanation: You can manage technical debt with SonarQube and Azure DevOps. Note: Technical debt is the set of problems in a development effort that make forward progress on customer value ineffcient. Technical debt saps productivity by making code hard to understand, fragile, time-consuming to change, diffcult to validate, and creates unplanned work that blocks progress. Unless they are managed, technical debt can accumulate and hurt the overall quality of the software and the productivity of the development ...

questions.topic: Topic 5 - During a code review, you discover many quality issues. Many modules contain unused variables and empty catch blocks.
questions.questionText: During a code review, you discover many quality issues. Many modules contain unused variables and empty catch blocks. You need to recommend a solution to improve the quality of the code. What should you recommend?
options: A. In a Grunt build task, select Enabled from Control Options.
B. In a Maven build task, select Run PMD.
C. In a Xcode build task, select Use xcpretty from Advanced.
questions.explanation: PMD is a source code analyzer. It finds common programming flaws like unused variables, empty catch blocks, unnecessary object creation, and so forth. There is an Apache Maven PMD Plugin which allows you to automatically run the PMD code analysis tool on your project's source code and generate a site report with its results. Incorrect Answers: C: xcpretty is a fast and flexible formatter for xcodebuild.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - Your development team is building a new web solution by using the Microsoft Visual Studio integrated development environment (IDE).
questions.questionText: Your development team is building a new web solution by using the Microsoft Visual Studio integrated development environment (IDE). You need to make a custom package available to all the developers. The package must be managed centrally, and the latest version must be available for consumption in Visual Studio automatically. Which three actions should you perform? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
options: A. Publish the package to a feed.
B. Create a new feed in Azure Artifacts.
C. Upload a package to a Git repository.
D. Add the package URL to the Environment settings in Visual Studio.
E. Add the package URL to the NuGet Package Manager settings in Visual Studio.
questions.explanation: B: By using your custom NuGet package feed within your Azure DevOps (previously VSTS) instance, you'll be able to distribute your packages within your organization with ease. Start by creating a new feed. A: We can publish, pack and push the built project to our NuGet feed. E: Consume your private NuGet Feed Go back to the Packages area in Azure DevOps, select your feed and hit "Connect to feed". You'll see some instructions for your feed, but it's fairly simple to set up. Just copy your packa...

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You use GitHub for source control.
questions.questionText: You use GitHub for source control. A file that contains sensitive data is committed accidentally to the Git repository of a project. You need to delete the file and its history form the repository. Which two tools can you use? Each correct answer presents a complete solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
questions.explanation: To entirely remove unwanted files from a repository's history you can use either the git filter-branch command or the BFG Repo-Cleaner open source tool.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - Your company uses GitHub for source control. The company has a team that performs code reviews.
questions.questionText: Your company uses GitHub for source control. The company has a team that performs code reviews. You need to automate the assignment of the code reviews. The solution must meet the following requirements: ✑ Prioritize the assignment of code reviews to team members who have the fewest outstanding assignments. ✑ Ensure that each team member performs an equal number of code reviews in any 30-day period. ✑ Prevent the assignment of code reviews to the team leader. Which two actions should you perform? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
options: A. Clear Never assign certain team members.
B. Select If assigning team members, don't notify the entire team.
C. Select Never assign certain team members.
D. Set Routing algorithm to Round robin.
questions.explanation: C: Select 'Never assign certain team members' and choose the team leader to prevent code reviews from being assigned to them. D: Set the Routing algorithm to 'Round robin' which distributes reviews equally and considers outstanding assignments, ensuring each team member performs an equal number of reviews in any 30-day period while prioritizing those with fewer outstanding assignments. Incorrect answers: A (Clear 'Never assign certain team members') is wrong because you need to enable this setting (select it) and exclude the team leader, not clear it. B (Select 'If assigning team members, don't notify the entire team') is wrong because this controls notifications, not the assignment algorithm.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have a GitHub repository.
questions.questionText: You have a GitHub repository. You create a new repository in Azure DevOps. You need to recommend a procedure to clone the repository from GitHub to Azure DevOps. What should you recommend?
options: C. Create a service connection for GitHub.
D. From Import a Git repository, click Import.
questions.explanation: You can import an existing Git repo from GitHub, Bitbucket, GitLab, or other location into a new or empty existing repo in your project in Azure DevOps. Import into a new repo - ✑ Select Repos, Files. ✑ From the repo drop-down, select Import repository. ✑ If the source repo is publicly available, just enter the clone URL of the source repository and a name for your new Git repository.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - During a code review, you discover quality issues in a Java application.
questions.questionText: During a code review, you discover quality issues in a Java application. You need to recommend a solution to detect quality issues including unused variables and empty catch blocks. What should you recommend?
options: A. In a Maven build task, select Run PMD.
B. In an Xcode build task, select Use xcpretty from Advanced.
C. In a Gulp build task, specify a custom condition expression.
questions.explanation: PMD is a source code analyzer. It finds common programming flaws like unused variables, empty catch blocks, unnecessary object creation, and so forth. There is an Apache Maven PMD Plugin which allows you to automatically run the PMD code analysis tool on your project's source code and generate a site report with its results. Incorrect Answers: B: xcpretty is a fast and flexible formatter for xcodebuild.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You use Azure Artifacts to host NuGet packages that you create.
questions.questionText: You use Azure Artifacts to host NuGet packages that you create. You need to make one of the packages available to anonymous users outside your organization. The solution must minimize the number of publication points. What should you do?
options: A. Change the feed URL of the package
B. Create a new feed for the package
C. Promote the package to a release view.
questions.explanation: Azure Artifacts introduces the concept of multiple feeds that you can use to organize and control access to your packages. Packages you host in Azure Artifacts are stored in a feed. Setting permissions on the feed allows you to share your packages with as many or as few people as your scenario requires. Feeds have four levels of access: Owners, Contributors, Collaborators, and Readers.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You use GitHub for source control and project-related discussions.
questions.questionText: You use GitHub for source control and project-related discussions. You receive a notiffcation when an entry is made to any team discussion. You need to ensure that you receive email notiffcations only for discussions in which you commented or in which you are mentioned. Which two Notiffcations settings should you clear? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
questions.explanation: To only receive notifications for discussions you commented on or were mentioned in, you need to: A) Clear 'Automatically watch teams' to stop receiving all team discussion notifications, and C) Clear 'Automatically watch repositories' to stop automatically watching new repositories. Incorrect answer: B (Participating) should not be cleared because the Participating notification setting is what ensures you receive notifications for discussions you commented on or were mentioned in - clearing it would prevent the desired notifications.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have an Azure Automation account that contains a runbook. The runbook is used to configure the application infrastructure of an Azure
questions.questionText: You have an Azure Automation account that contains a runbook. The runbook is used to configure the application infrastructure of an Azure subscription. You have a project in Azure DevOps named Project1. Project1 contains a repository that stores code for the runbook. You need to ensure that every committed change to the code will update automatically and publish the runbook to Azure Automation. What should you configure?
options: A. the Service hooks settings for Project1
B. the Connections settings for the Automation account
C. the Source control settings for the Automation account
questions.explanation: The Source control settings for the Azure Automation account should be configured to automatically sync and publish runbooks from the Azure DevOps repository. Azure Automation source control integration allows you to associate a repository with your Automation account and automatically sync changes. Incorrect answers: A (Service hooks for Project1) is wrong because service hooks send notifications to external services but do not directly publish runbooks. B (Connections settings) is wrong because Connections in Azure Automation manage credentials and connection assets for runbooks, not source control synchronization.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You use Git for source control.
questions.questionText: You use Git for source control. You enable GitHub code scanning. You raise a pull request from a non-default branch. In the code scanning output, you receive the following error message: “Analysis not found.” You need to ensure that the code scanning completes successfully for the pull request. Which two actions should you perform? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
options: A. Add the name of the default branch to the on: push speciffcation in the code scanning workflow.
B. Add the name of the non-default branch to the on:push speciffcation in the code scanning workflow.
C. Delete the pull request, and then raise the request again from the default branch.
D. Update the code in the pull request.
questions.explanation: To resolve the 'Analysis not found' error for code scanning on a non-default branch pull request: A) Add the name of the default branch to the on:push specification so the code scanning workflow runs on the default branch (this creates the baseline analysis). D) Update the code in the pull request to trigger a new analysis on the non-default branch. Code scanning requires a baseline analysis on the default branch before it can compare results from pull requests. Incorrect answers: B (add non-default branch to on:push) is wrong because the issue is the missing baseline on the default branch, not the non-default branch configuration. C (delete and recreate from default) is wrong because this changes the source of the PR rather than fixing the scanning configuration.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You use GitHub for source control and Microsoft Teams for collaboration.
questions.questionText: You use GitHub for source control and Microsoft Teams for collaboration. You need to send a notiffcation to a Teams channel for each commit. The solution must minimize development effort. What should you do?
options: A. Use Azure Automation to connect to the GitHub Actions API and send a message to the Teams channel.
B. Use the Microsoft Teams for GitHub app and configure a subscription to receive notiffcations in the Teams channel.
C. Use GitHub Actions with a dispatch to send a message to the Teams channel by using the Teams API.
questions.explanation: Using the Microsoft Teams for GitHub app and configuring a subscription to receive notifications in the Teams channel is the solution that minimizes development effort. This app provides built-in integration between GitHub and Teams with no custom code required. Incorrect answers: A (Azure Automation with GitHub Actions API) is wrong because it requires significant development effort to build and maintain. C (GitHub Actions with dispatch to Teams API) is wrong because it requires writing custom workflow code to interact with the Teams API.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You manage source code control and versioning by using GitHub.
questions.questionText: You manage source code control and versioning by using GitHub. You need to ensure that a PowerShell script is executed automatically before rebase operations are performed. What should you use?
questions.explanation: A webhook is not the correct answer for executing a local script before rebase operations. The correct mechanism is Git hooks, specifically the pre-rebase hook. However, among the given options, 'webhook' is listed. Note: In the context of this exam, the answer refers to Git hooks (client-side hooks), not web webhooks. The pre-rebase hook runs before a rebase operation begins. Incorrect answers: A (a package) is wrong because packages are for distributing code libraries, not for executing scripts on Git events. B (GitHub Copilot) is wrong because it is an AI coding assistant, not a mechanism for running scripts on Git events.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You use GitHub to host container packages that use Semantic Versioning (SemVer).
questions.questionText: You use GitHub to host container packages that use Semantic Versioning (SemVer). You have an app named App1. The current version of App1 is 11.2.0. You change the code of App1 to fix a bug that was introduced in version 10.5.1. Which version number should you assign to the release?
questions.explanation: In Semantic Versioning (SemVer), when you fix a bug, you increment the PATCH version. Since the current version is 11.2.0, fixing a bug (even one introduced in an earlier version) results in version 11.2.1. The version always moves forward - you do not go back to modify old version numbers. Incorrect answers: A (10.5.1-PATCH) is wrong because SemVer does not use suffixes like '-PATCH' and you cannot release a version lower than the current version. C (10.5.2) is wrong because you cannot release a version number lower than the current version (11.2.0).

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You manage code by using GitHub.
questions.questionText: You manage code by using GitHub. You need to ensure that repository owners are notiffed if a new vulnerable dependency or malware is found in their repository. What should you do?
options: C. Configure branch protection rules for each repository.
questions.explanation: Dependabot alerts automatically notify repository owners when a new vulnerable dependency or malware is found in their repository. Dependabot monitors your dependency manifests and compares them against known vulnerability databases. Incorrect answers: A (CodeQL scanning actions) is wrong because CodeQL performs static code analysis on your own code, not dependency vulnerability scanning. C (branch protection rules) is wrong because branch protection rules enforce workflow requirements on branches (like reviews and status checks), not vulnerability detection.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have a public GitHub repository named Public1.
questions.questionText: You have a public GitHub repository named Public1. A commit is made to Public1. The commit contains a pattern that matches a regular expression. Who is notiffed first when the commit is made?
options: A. the administrator of the GitHub organization
questions.explanation: When a commit containing a pattern matching a secret is pushed to a public GitHub repository, the repository owner (and people with admin access) are notified first via email. For public repositories, GitHub also notifies the relevant service provider (secret scanning partner) if the pattern matches a partner pattern. Incorrect answers: A (administrator of the GitHub organization) is wrong because the repository owner is notified first, not the organization administrator. B (the committer) is wrong because the committer is not the first to be notified - the repository owner and admins receive the alert first.

questions.topic: Topic 6 - Your company has 60 developers who are assigned to four teams. Each team has 15 members.
questions.questionText: Your company has 60 developers who are assigned to four teams. Each team has 15 members. The company uses an agile development methodology. You need to structure the work of the development teams so that each team owns their respective work while working together to reach a common goal. Which parts of the taxonomy should you enable the team to perform autonomously?
questions.explanation: A feature typically represents a shippable component of software. Features, examples: ✑ Add view options to the new work hub ✑ Add mobile shopping cart ✑ Support text alerts ✑ Refresh the web portal with new look and feel User Stories and Tasks are used to track work. Teams can choose how they track bugs, either as requirements or as tasks Incorrect Answers: B, C: An epic represents a business initiative to be accomplished. Epics, examples: ✑ Increase customer engagement ✑ Improve and simplify t...

questions.topic: Topic 6 - Your company creates a new Azure DevOps team.
questions.questionText: Your company creates a new Azure DevOps team. You plan to use Azure DevOps for sprint planning. You need to visualize the flow of your work by using an agile methodology. Which Azure DevOps component should you use?
questions.explanation: Customizing Kanban boards. To maximize a team's ability to consistently deliver high quality software, Kanban emphasize two main practices. The first, visualize the flow of work, requires you to map your team's workflow stages and configure your Kanban board to match. Your Kanban board turns your backlog into an interactive signboard, providing a visual flow of work.

questions.topic: Topic 6 - Your company implements an Agile development methodology.
questions.questionText: Your company implements an Agile development methodology. You plan to implement retrospectives at the end of each sprint. Which three questions should you include? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
options: B. Who should have performed better?
C. What could have gone better?
questions.explanation: Sprint retrospectives focus on process improvement using three key questions: 'What went well?' (D), 'What could have gone better?' (C), and 'What will we change for next time?' These questions focus on processes and outcomes, not individual performance. Incorrect answers: A ('Who performed well?') is wrong because retrospectives focus on processes and teamwork, not individual performance evaluation. B ('Who should have performed better?') is wrong because singling out individuals goes against the collaborative spirit of retrospectives and can create a blame culture.

questions.topic: Topic 6 - You plan to create in Azure DevOps. Multiple developers will work on the project. The developers will work offine frequently and will require
questions.questionText: You plan to create in Azure DevOps. Multiple developers will work on the project. The developers will work offine frequently and will require access to the full project history while they are offine. Which version control solution should you use?
questions.explanation: Git history: File history is replicated on the client dev machine and can be viewed even when not connected to the server. You can view history in Visual Studio and on the web portal. Note: Azure Repos supports two types of version control: Git and Team Foundation Version Control (TFVC). Incorrect Answers: A: Team Foundation Version Control: File history is not replicated on the client dev machine and so can be viewed only when you're connected to the server.

questions.topic: Topic 6 - You have an Azure DevOps organization named Contoso and an Azure subscription.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure DevOps organization named Contoso and an Azure subscription. You use Azure DevOps to build and deploy a web app named App1. Azure Monitor is configured to generate an email notiffcation in response to alerts generated whenever App1 generates a server-side error. You need to receive notiffcations in Microsoft Teams whenever an Azure Monitor alert is generated. Which two actions should you perform? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
options: A. Create an Azure Monitor workbook.
B. Create an Azure logic app that has an HTTP request trigger.
C. Create an Azure logic app that has an Azure DevOps trigger.
D. Modify an action group in Azure Monitor.
questions.explanation: B: Create an Azure Logic App with an HTTP request trigger that can receive webhook calls from Azure Monitor and then send notifications to Microsoft Teams. D: Modify the action group in Azure Monitor to add a webhook action that calls the Logic App's HTTP endpoint when an alert fires. Incorrect answers: A (Azure Monitor workbook) is wrong because workbooks are for data visualization, not for sending notifications. C (Logic App with Azure DevOps trigger) is wrong because the notifications need to be triggered by Azure Monitor alerts, not by Azure DevOps events.

questions.topic: Topic 6 - You have an Azure DevOps organization that contains a project named Project1.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure DevOps organization that contains a project named Project1. You need to create a published wiki in Project1. What should you do first?
options: A. Modify the Storage settings of Project1.
B. In Project1, create an Azure DevOps pipeline.
C. In Project1, create an Azure DevOps repository.
questions.explanation: To create a published wiki in Azure DevOps, you first need a Git repository because published wikis are backed by Git repositories. You can publish an existing Git repository (or a folder within it) as a wiki. Incorrect answers: A (Modify Storage settings) is wrong because storage settings do not relate to wiki creation. B (Create a pipeline) is wrong because pipelines are for CI/CD, not for wiki publishing.

questions.topic: Topic 6 - You are designing a YAML template for use with Azure Pipelines. The template will include the outputfile parameter.
questions.questionText: You are designing a YAML template for use with Azure Pipelines. The template will include the outputfile parameter. Which two methods can you use to reference the parameter? Each correct answer presents a complete solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
questions.explanation: In Azure Pipelines YAML templates, parameters are referenced using the template expression syntax ${{parameters.parameterName}}. This is the only valid syntax for referencing template parameters. Template expressions are processed at pipeline compile time before the pipeline runs. Incorrect answers: B, C, and D are wrong because the $(variable) syntax is used for runtime variables, not for template parameters. Parameters require the ${{ }} template expression syntax.

questions.topic: Topic 6 - You have a pipeline named Pipeline1 in Azure Pipelines.
questions.questionText: You have a pipeline named Pipeline1 in Azure Pipelines. You need to create a service connection to enable Pipeline1 to download a public container image. Which type of service connection should you create?
questions.explanation: A Docker registry service connection is needed to connect to a container registry (such as Docker Hub, Azure Container Registry, or any other Docker V2-compatible registry) to pull or push container images. Incorrect answers: A (Docker host) is wrong because a Docker host service connection connects to a Docker daemon for running containers, not for downloading images from a registry. C (Azure Service Fabric) is wrong because it is a service connection for deploying to Service Fabric clusters, not for accessing container registries.

questions.topic: Topic 6 - You have an Azure subscription that contains a storage account and 20 virtual machines.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains a storage account and 20 virtual machines. You plan to use LogRhythm for aggregation and analysis of the virtual machine logs. You need to configure AzLog to export the logs and push them to the storage account. In which format should you export the logs?
questions.explanation: Azure diagnostic logs and activity logs should be exported in JSON format when pushing to a storage account for integration with third-party tools like LogRhythm. JSON is the standard format for Azure log data and is widely supported by log aggregation and analysis tools. Incorrect answers: B (EVTX) is wrong because EVTX is the Windows Event Log format, used locally on Windows systems, not for Azure log export. C (EVT) is wrong because EVT is the legacy Windows Event Log format, also not applicable for Azure log export.

questions.topic: Topic 6 - You use GitHub for source control and Azure Boards for project management. GitHub and Azure Boards are integrated.
questions.questionText: You use GitHub for source control and Azure Boards for project management. GitHub and Azure Boards are integrated. You plan to create a pull request in GitHub. You need to automatically link the request to an existing Azure Boards work item by using the text of AB#. To which two elements can you add the text? Each correct answer presents a complete solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
questions.explanation: When integrating GitHub with Azure Boards, you can use the AB#{ID} syntax in pull request titles (C) and comments/descriptions (D) to automatically link to Azure Boards work items. The AB# syntax is recognized in commit messages, pull request titles, and pull request descriptions/comments. Incorrect answers: A (milestone) is wrong because AB# syntax is not processed in milestone fields. B (label) is wrong because labels are tags/categories and do not process AB# syntax.

questions.topic: Topic 6 - You are integrating an Azure Boards project and a GitHub repository.
questions.questionText: You are integrating an Azure Boards project and a GitHub repository. You need to authenticate Azure Boards to GitHub. Which two authentication methods can you use? Each correct answer presents a complete solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
options: C. Microsoft Azure Active Directory (Azure AD), part of Microsoft Entra
questions.explanation: Azure Boards can authenticate to GitHub using either: C) OAuth through Azure AD (Microsoft Entra) authentication, or D) GitHub user credentials (personal access token). Both methods are supported for connecting Azure Boards to GitHub repositories. Incorrect answers: A (publisher certificate) is wrong because certificates are not used for Azure Boards to GitHub authentication. B (trusted root certificate) is wrong for the same reason.

questions.topic: Topic 6 - You have a GitHub repository that is integrated with Azure Boards. Azure Boards has a work item that has the number 715.
questions.questionText: You have a GitHub repository that is integrated with Azure Boards. Azure Boards has a work item that has the number 715. You need to ensure that when you commit source code in GitHub, the work item is updated automatically. What should you include in the commit comments?
options: A. the URL of the work item
questions.explanation: Using AB#715 in the commit comments will automatically create a link between the GitHub commit and Azure Boards work item #715. The AB# prefix followed by the work item ID is the standard syntax for linking GitHub commits to Azure Boards work items. Incorrect answers: A (URL of the work item) is wrong because simply pasting a URL in the commit message does not create an automatic link in Azure Boards. C (@715) is wrong because the @ syntax is used for mentioning users in GitHub, not for linking to Azure Boards work items.

questions.topic: Topic 6 - You are creating a dashboard in Azure Boards.
questions.questionText: You are creating a dashboard in Azure Boards. You need to visualize the time from when work starts on a work item until the work item is closed. Which type of widget should you use?
questions.explanation: The Cycle Time widget measures the time from when work begins on a work item (moves to active/in-progress state) until it is completed/closed. This is exactly what the question asks for - visualizing the time from when work starts until the work item is closed. Incorrect answers: B (velocity) is wrong because velocity measures the amount of work completed in each sprint/iteration, not the time to complete individual work items. C (cumulative flow) is wrong because the cumulative flow diagram shows the count of work items in various states over time, not the duration of individual work items.

questions.topic: Topic 6 - You manage a project by using Azure Boards. You manage the project code by using GitHub.
questions.questionText: You manage a project by using Azure Boards. You manage the project code by using GitHub. You have three work items that have IDs of 456, 457, and 458. You need to create a pull request that will be linked to all the work items. The solution must set the state of work item 456 to done. What should you add to the commit message?
questions.explanation: Using 'Fixes #AB456, #AB457, #AB458' links all three work items and the 'Fixes' keyword sets work item 456 to done/completed state. The Fixes keyword combined with the AB# syntax triggers automatic state transitions in Azure Boards. Note: The exact syntax may vary, but AB# prefix with Fixes keyword is the correct approach. Incorrect answers: A (Fixes #456, #457, #458) is wrong because without the AB prefix, these refer to GitHub issues, not Azure Boards work items. C (#456, #457, #458 Completed #456) is wrong because this is not valid Azure Boards linking syntax.

questions.topic: Topic 7 - You have a project in Azure DevOps.
questions.questionText: You have a project in Azure DevOps. You create the following YAML template named Template1.yml. steps: - script: npm install - script: yarn install - script: npm run compile You create the following pipeline named File1.yml. parameters: usersteps: - task: MyTask@1 - script: echo Done You need to ensure that Template1.yaml runs before File1.yml. How should you update File1.yml?
options: A. parameters: usersteps: extends: template: template1.yml - task: MyTask@1 - script: echo Done
B. template: template1.yml parameters: usersteps: - task: MyTask@1 - script: echo Done
C. extends: template: templatel.yml parameters: usersteps: - task: MyTask@1 - script: echo Done
questions.explanation: Azure Pipelines offers two kinds of templates: includes and extends. Included templates behave like #include in C++: it's as if you paste the template's code right into the outer file, which references it. To continue the C++ metaphor, extends templates are more like inheritance: the template provides the outer structure of the pipeline and a set of places where the template consumer can make targeted alterations. Example: extends: template: template.yml@templates parameters: usersteps: - script:...

questions.topic: Topic 7 - You store source code in a Git repository in Azure Repos. You use a third-party continuous integration (CI) tool to control builds.
questions.questionText: You store source code in a Git repository in Azure Repos. You use a third-party continuous integration (CI) tool to control builds. What will Azure DevOps use to authenticate with the tool?
options: C. a Shared Access Signature (SAS) token
questions.explanation: Personal access tokens (PATs) give you access to Azure DevOps and Team Foundation Server (TFS), without using your username and password directly.

questions.topic: Topic 7 - Your company uses a Git repository in Azure Repos to manage the source code of a web application. The master branch is protected from direct
questions.questionText: Your company uses a Git repository in Azure Repos to manage the source code of a web application. The master branch is protected from direct updates. Developers work on new features in the topic branches. Because of the high volume of requested features, it is diffcult to follow the history of the changes to the master branch. You need to enforce a pull request merge strategy. The strategy must meet the following requirements: ✑ Consolidate commit histories. ✑ Merge the changes into a single commit. Which merge strategy should you use in the branch policy?
questions.explanation: Squash merging is a merge option that allows you to condense the Git history of topic branches when you complete a pull request. Instead of each commit on the topic branch being added to the history of the default branch, a squash merge takes all the file changes and adds them to a single new commit on the default branch. A simple way to think about this is that squash merge gives you just the file changes, and a regular merge gives you the file changes and the commit history. Note: Squash merging ...

questions.topic: Topic 7 - Your company uses cloud-hosted Jenkins for builds.
questions.questionText: Your company uses cloud-hosted Jenkins for builds. You need to ensure that Jenkins can retrieve source code from Azure Repos. Which three actions should you perform? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
options: A. Create a webhook in Jenkins.
B. Add the Team Foundation Server (TFS) plug-in to Jenkins.
C. Add a personal access token to your Jenkins account.
D. Create a personal access token (PAT) in your Azure DevOps account.
questions.explanation: B: Jenkins requires a plug-in to connect to TFS and check for updates to a project. Jenkins' built-in Git Plugin or Team Foundation Server Plugin can poll a Team Services repository every few minutes and queue a job when changes are detected. C: Use Azure DevOps/ Visual Studio Team Services to create a Personal access token. D: After you have generated credentials using Visual Studio Team Services, you need to use those credentials in Jenkins.

questions.topic: Topic 7 - You are automating the build process for a Java-based application by using Azure DevOps.
questions.questionText: You are automating the build process for a Java-based application by using Azure DevOps. You need to add code coverage testing and publish the outcomes to the pipeline. What should you use?
questions.explanation: Use Publish Code Coverage Results task in a build pipeline to publish code coverage results to Azure Pipelines or TFS, which were produced by a build in Cobertura or JaCoCo format. Incorrect Answers: A: Bullseye Coverage is used for C++ code, and not for Java. Note: There are several versions of this question in the exam. The question has two possible correct answers: ✑ Cobertura ✑ JaCoCo Other incorrect answer options you may see on the exam include the following: ✑ Coverlet ✑ NUnit ✑ Coverage....

questions.topic: Topic 7 - You are automating the build process for a Java-based application by using Azure DevOps.
questions.questionText: You are automating the build process for a Java-based application by using Azure DevOps. You need to add code coverage testing and publish the outcomes to the pipeline. What should you use?
questions.explanation: Use Publish Code Coverage Results task in a build pipeline to publish code coverage results to Azure Pipelines or TFS, which were produced by a build in Cobertura or JaCoCo format. Incorrect Answers: B: Bullseye Coverage is used for C++ code, and not for Java. D: If you're building on Linux or macOS, you can use Coverlet or a similar tool to collect code coverage metrics. Code coverage results can be published to the server by using the Publish Code Coverage Results task. To leverage this functi...

questions.topic: Topic 7 - You have an existing build pipeline in Azure Pipelines.
questions.questionText: You have an existing build pipeline in Azure Pipelines. You need to use incremental builds without purging the environment between pipeline executions. What should you use?
questions.explanation: When you run a pipeline on a self-hosted agent, by default, none of the subdirectories are cleaned in between two consecutive runs. As a result, you can do incremental builds and deployments, provided that tasks are implemented to make use of that. You can override this behavior using the workspace setting on the job. Incorrect Answers: B: The workspace clean options are applicable only for self-hosted agents. When using Microsoft-hosted agents job are always run on a new agent.

questions.topic: Topic 7 - Your company has a project in Azure DevOps for a new application. The application will be deployed to several Azure virtual machines that run
questions.questionText: Your company has a project in Azure DevOps for a new application. The application will be deployed to several Azure virtual machines that run Windows Server 2019. You need to recommend a deployment strategy for the virtual machines. The strategy must meet the following requirements: ✑ Ensure that the virtual machines maintain a consistent configuration. ✑ Minimize administrative effort to configure the virtual machines. What should you include in the recommendation?
options: A. Azure Resource Manager templates and the PowerShell Desired State Configuration (DSC) extension for Windows
B. Deployment YAML and Azure pipeline deployment groups
C. Azure Resource Manager templates and the Custom Script Extension for Windows
questions.explanation: The Custom Script Extension downloads and executes scripts on Azure virtual machines. This extension is useful for post deployment configuration, software installation, or any other configuration or management tasks. Scripts can be downloaded from Azure storage or GitHub, or provided to the Azure portal at extension run time. The Custom Script Extension integrates with Azure Resource Manager templates, and can be run using the Azure CLI, PowerShell, Azure portal, or the Azure Virtual Machine REST ...

questions.topic: Topic 7 - You have an Azure DevOps project that uses many package feeds.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure DevOps project that uses many package feeds. You need to simplify the project by using a single feed that stores packages produced by your company and packages consumed from remote feeds. The solution must support public feeds and authenticated feeds. What should you enable in DevOps?
questions.explanation: Upstream sources enable you to use a single feed to store both the packages you produce and the packages you consume from "remote feeds". This includes both public feeds, such as npmjs.com and nuget.org, and authenticated feeds, such as other Azure DevOps feeds in your organization. Once you've enabled an upstream source, any user connected to your feed can install a package from the remote feed, and your feed will save a copy.

questions.topic: Topic 7 - You use a Git repository in Azure Repos to manage the source code of a web application. Developers commit changes directly to the default
questions.questionText: You use a Git repository in Azure Repos to manage the source code of a web application. Developers commit changes directly to the default branch. You need to implement a change management procedure that meets the following requirements: ✑ The default branch must be protected, and new changes must be built in the feature branches first. ✑ Changes must be reviewed and approved by at least one release manager before each merge. ✑ Changes must be brought into the default branch by using pull requests. What should you configure in Azure Repos?
options: A. branch policies of the default branch
C. Deployment pools in Project Settings
questions.explanation: Branch policies help teams protect their important branches of development. Policies enforce your team's code quality and change management standards.

questions.topic: Topic 7 - You need to recommend a Docker container build strategy that meets the following requirements:
questions.questionText: You need to recommend a Docker container build strategy that meets the following requirements: ✑ Minimizes image sizes ✑ Minimizes the security surface area of the final image What should you include in the recommendation?
questions.explanation: Multi-stage builds are a new feature requiring Docker 17.05 or higher on the daemon and client. Multistage builds are useful to anyone who has struggled to optimize Dockerfiles while keeping them easy to read and maintain. Incorrect Answers: C: A swarm consists of multiple Docker hosts which run in swarm mode and act as managers (to manage membership and delegation) and workers (which run swarm services).

questions.topic: Topic 7 - You plan to create an image that will contain a .NET Core application.
questions.questionText: You plan to create an image that will contain a .NET Core application. You have a Dockerfile file that contains the following code. (Line numbers are included for reference only.) You need to ensure that the image is as small as possible when the image is built. Which line should you modify in the file?
questions.explanation: Multi-stage builds (in Docker 17.05 or higher) allow you to drastically reduce the size of your final image, without struggling to reduce the number of intermediate layers and files. With multi-stage builds, you use multiple FROM statements in your Dockerfile. Each FROM instruction can use a different base, and each of them begins a new stage of the build. You can selectively copy artifacts from one stage to another, leaving behind everything you don't want in the final image.

questions.topic: Topic 7 - You have 50 Node.js-based projects that you scan by using WhiteSource. Each project includes Package.json, Package-lock.json, and Npm-
questions.questionText: You have 50 Node.js-based projects that you scan by using WhiteSource. Each project includes Package.json, Package-lock.json, and Npm- shrinkwrap.json files. You need to minimize the number of libraries reports by WhiteSource to only the libraries that you explicitly reference. What should you do?
options: A. Configure the File System Agent plug-in.
B. Add a devDependencies section to Package-lock.json.
questions.explanation: Separate Your Dependencies - Within your package.json file be sure you split out your npm dependencies between devDependencies and (production) dependencies. The key part is that you must then make use of the --production flag when installing the npm packages. The --production flag will exclude all packages defined in the devDependencies section.

questions.topic: Topic 7 - Your company deploys applications in Docker containers.
questions.questionText: Your company deploys applications in Docker containers. You want to detect known exploits in the Docker images used to provision the Docker containers. You need to integrate image scanning into the application lifecycle. The solution must expose the exploits as early as possible during the application lifecycle. What should you configure?
options: A. a task executed in the continuous integration pipeline and a scheduled task that analyzes the image registry
B. manual tasks performed during the planning phase and the deployment phase
C. a task executed in the continuous deployment pipeline and a scheduled task against a running production container
questions.explanation: You can use the Docker task to sign into ACR and then use a subsequent script to pull an image and scan the container image for vulnerabilities. Use the docker task in a build or release pipeline. This task can be used with Docker or Azure Container registry. Incorrect Answers: C: We should not wait until deployment. We want to detect the exploits as early as possible. D: We should wait until the image is in the product container. We want to detect the exploits as early as possible.

questions.topic: Topic 7 - Your company has an Azure DevOps project,
questions.questionText: Your company has an Azure DevOps project, The source code for the project is stored in an on-premises repository and uses on an on-premises build server. You plan to use Azure DevOps to control the build process on the build server by using a self-hosted agent. You need to implement the self-hosted agent. You download and install the agent on the build server. Which two actions should you perform next? Each correct answer presents part of the solution.
options: A. From Azure, create a shared access signature (SAS).
B. From the build server, create a certiffcate, and then upload the certiffcate to Azure Storage.
C. From the build server, create a certiffcate, and then upload the certiffcate to Azure Key Vault.
D. From DevOps, create a personal access token (PAT).
questions.explanation: After downloading and installing the self-hosted agent, you need to: D) Create a personal access token (PAT) in Azure DevOps to authenticate the agent. B) Configure the agent on the build server, which may include handling SSL certificates if using HTTPS. The PAT is used during agent configuration to register it with the Azure DevOps organization. Incorrect answers: A (create a SAS) is wrong because Shared Access Signatures are for Azure Storage access, not for agent authentication. C (certificate to Key Vault) is wrong because the agent registration uses PAT authentication, not certificates stored in Key Vault.

questions.topic: Topic 7 - You have an Azure subscription that contains an Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) tenant.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains an Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) tenant. You are configuring a build pipeline in Azure Pipelines that will include a task named Task1. Task1 will authenticate by using an Azure AD service principal. Which three values should you configure for Task1? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
questions.explanation: When configuring a task to authenticate using an Azure AD service principal, you need three key values: A) the tenant ID (identifies the Azure AD directory), C) the client secret (the password/key for the service principal), and D) the app/client ID (identifies the service principal application). Incorrect answer: B (subscription ID) is needed for the service connection configuration but is not part of the service principal authentication itself - though in practice, you often need all four values.

questions.topic: Topic 7 - You are developing an open source solution that uses a GitHub repository.
questions.questionText: You are developing an open source solution that uses a GitHub repository. You create a new public project in Azure DevOps. You plan to use Azure Pipelines for continuous build. The solution will use the GitHub Checks API. Which authentication type should you use?
questions.explanation: Write permission for the Checks API is only available to GitHub Apps. Note: Authenticating as a GitHub App lets you do a couple of things: ✑ You can retrieve high-level management information about your GitHub App. ✑ You can request access tokens for an installation of the app.

questions.topic: Topic 7 - You have an Azure DevOps organization named Contoso and an Azure DevOps project named Project1.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure DevOps organization named Contoso and an Azure DevOps project named Project1. You plan to use Microsoft-hosted agents to build container images that will host full Microsoft .NET Framework apps in a YAML pipeline in Project1. What are two possible virtual machine images that you can use for the Microsoft-hosted agent pool? Each correct answer presents a complete solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
questions.explanation: To build container images hosting full .NET Framework apps, you need a Windows-based agent. A) vs2017-win2016 provides a Windows Server 2016 environment with Visual Studio 2017 and Docker support. C) win1803 provides Windows Server 1803 specifically designed for running Windows containers. Full .NET Framework requires Windows. Incorrect answers: B (ubuntu-16.04) is wrong because Linux cannot run full .NET Framework applications. D (macOS-10.13) is wrong because macOS cannot run Windows containers needed for .NET Framework.

questions.topic: Topic 7 - Your company has a project in Azure DevOps.
questions.questionText: Your company has a project in Azure DevOps. You need to ensure that when there are multiple builds pending deployment, only the most recent build is deployed. What should you use?
questions.explanation: The options you can choose for a queuing policy are: ✑ Number of parallel deployments ✑ If you specify a maximum number of deployments, two more options appear: - Deploy all in sequence - Deploy latest and cancel the others: Use this option if you are producing releases faster than builds, and you only want to deploy the latest build. Incorrect Answers: C: Release gates allow automatic collection of health signals from external services, and then promote the release when all the signals are succ...

questions.topic: Topic 7 - Your company develops a client banking application that processes a large volume of data.
questions.questionText: Your company develops a client banking application that processes a large volume of data. Code quality is an ongoing issue for the company. Recently, the code quality has deteriorated because of an increase in time pressure on the development team. You need to implement static code analysis. During which phase should you use static code analysis?
questions.explanation: The Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) Guidelines recommend that teams perform static analysis during the implementation phase of their development cycle. Note: The company should focus in particular on the implementation of DevOps tests to assess the quality of the software from the planning stage to the implementation phase of the project.

questions.topic: Topic 7 - You are developing an application. The application source has multiple branches.
questions.questionText: You are developing an application. The application source has multiple branches. You make several changes to a branch used for experimentation. You need to update the main branch to capture the changes made to the experimentation branch and override the history of the Git repository. Which Git option should you use?
questions.explanation: Rebase is the correct option when you need to update a branch and override (rewrite) the Git history. Rebase replays commits from one branch onto another, creating new commits and rewriting the history. This makes it appear as if the changes were made on top of the target branch. Incorrect answers: B (Fetch) is wrong because fetch only downloads commits from a remote repository without modifying local branches or history. C (Merge) is wrong because merge creates a new merge commit and preserves the original commit history rather than overriding it.

questions.topic: Topic 7 - You have a project in Azure DevOps named Project1. Project1 contains a pipeline that builds a container image named Image1 and pushes Image1
questions.questionText: You have a project in Azure DevOps named Project1. Project1 contains a pipeline that builds a container image named Image1 and pushes Image1 to an Azure container registry named ACR1. Image1 uses a base image stored in Docker Hub. You need to ensure that Image1 is updated automatically whenever the base image is updated. What should you do?
options: A. Enable the Azure Event Grid resource provider and subscribe to registry events.
B. Add a Docker Hub service connection to Azure Pipelines.
C. Create and run an Azure Container Registry task.
questions.explanation: ACR Tasks supports automated container image builds when a container's base image is updated, such as when you patch the OS or application framework in one of your base images.

questions.topic: Topic 7 - Your company is building a new solution in Java.
questions.questionText: Your company is building a new solution in Java. The company currently uses a SonarQube server to analyze the code of .NET solutions. You need to analyze and monitor the code quality of the Java solution. Which task types should you add to the build pipeline?
questions.explanation: The SonarScanner for Maven is recommended as the default scanner for Maven projects. The ability to execute the SonarQube analysis via a regular Maven goal makes it available anywhere Maven is available (developer build, CI server, etc.), without the need to manually download, setup, and maintain a SonarQube Runner installation. The Maven build already has much of the information needed for SonarQube to successfully analyze a project. By preconfiguring the analysis based on that information, the ...

questions.topic: Topic 7 - You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1 that contains a custom Azure policy named Policy1. Policy1 is an audit policy that monitors
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1 that contains a custom Azure policy named Policy1. Policy1 is an audit policy that monitors naming convention compliance for the resources deployed to Subscription1. You have a pipeline named Pipeline1 in Azure Pipelines. Pipeline1 deploys Azure Resource Manager (ARM) resources to Subscription1. You need to ensure that the resources deployed by Pipeline1 comply with Policy1. What should you add to Pipeline1?
options: A. a pre-deployment task that runs a security and compliance assessment
B. a post-deployment task that runs a security and compliance assessment
C. an ARM template deployment task to assign Policy1 to Subscription1
questions.explanation: Check policy compliance with gates. Prepare - 1. Create an Azure Policy in the Azure portal. There are several predefined sample policies that can be applied to a management group, subscription, and resource group. 2. In Azure DevOps create a release pipeline that contains at least one stage, or open an existing release pipeline. 3. Add a pre- or post-deployment condition that includes the Security and compliance assessment task as a gate.

questions.topic: Topic 7 - You have a project in Azure DevOps.
questions.questionText: You have a project in Azure DevOps. You need to push notiffcations about pull requests to a Microsoft Teams channel. The solution must minimize development effort. What should you do?
options: A. Install the Azure Pipelines app for Teams and configure a subscription to receive notiffcations in the channel.
B. Use Azure Automation to connect to the Azure DevOps REST API and send messages to Teams.
C. Install the Azure Repos app for Teams and configure a subscription to receive notiffcations in the channel.
questions.explanation: If you use Microsoft Teams, you can use the Azure Repos app for Teams to easily monitor your repositories. Set up and manage subscriptions to receive notiffcations in your channel whenever code is pushed/checked in or when a pull request (PR) is created, updated or merged. The app supports monitoring both Git and Team Foundation Version Control (TFVC) repositories.

questions.topic: Topic 7 - You have an Azure subscription that contains the resources shown in the following table.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains the resources shown in the following table. Project produces npm packages that are published to Feed1. Feed1 is consumed by multiple projects. You need to ensure that only tested packages are available for consumption. The solution must minimize development effort. What should you do?
options: A. Create a feed view named @release and set @release as the default view. After the npm packages test successfully, configure a release pipeline that promotes a package to the @release view.
B. Create a feed view named @release and set @release as the default view. After the npm packages test successfully, configure a release pipeline that tags the packages as release.
C. Create a feed view named @default. After the npm packages test successfully, configure a release pipeline that tags the packages as release.
questions.explanation: Creating a feed view named @release and setting it as the default view ensures that only promoted (tested) packages are visible to consumers by default. After packages pass testing, a release pipeline promotes them to the @release view, making them available for consumption. Incorrect answers: B (tag packages as release) is wrong because tagging alone does not control visibility in the default view. C (feed view named @default) is wrong because the @default view cannot be created manually and tagging alone does not provide the same visibility control as view promotion.

questions.topic: Topic 7 - Your company has an Azure DevOps project that produces Node Package Manager (npm) packages. Multiple projects consume the packages.
questions.questionText: Your company has an Azure DevOps project that produces Node Package Manager (npm) packages. Multiple projects consume the packages. You need to minimize the amount of disk space used by older packages in Azure Artifacts. What should you modify?
options: A. the retention settings of the project’s release
B. the retention settings of the project’s pipeline
C. the retention settings of the project’s tests
questions.explanation: Modifying the retention settings of the project's pipeline controls how long build artifacts (including packages) are kept. By reducing the retention period, older packages that are no longer needed will be automatically cleaned up, minimizing disk space usage in Azure Artifacts. Incorrect answers: A (retention settings of release) is wrong because release retention controls release history, not package storage. C (retention settings of tests) is wrong because test retention controls test results, not package storage.

questions.topic: Topic 8 - Your company uses Azure DevOps to manage the build and release processes for applications.
questions.questionText: Your company uses Azure DevOps to manage the build and release processes for applications. You use a Git repository for applications source control. You plan to create a new branch from an existing pull request. Later, you plan to merge the new branch and the target branch of the pull request. You need to use a pull request action to create the new branch. The solution must ensure that the branch uses only a portion of the code in the pull request. Which pull request action should you use?
questions.explanation: Cherry-pick a pull request - To copy changes made in a pull request to another branch in your repo, follow these steps: 1. In a completed pull request, select Cherry-pick, or for an active pull request, select Cherry-pick from the ... menu. Cherry-picking a pull request in this way creates a new branch with the copied changes. Merge into a target branch in a second pull request. 2. In Target branch, enter the branch you want to merge the copied changes. 3. In Topic branch name, enter a new branc...

questions.topic: Topic 8 - You are designing a build pipeline in Azure Pipelines.
questions.questionText: You are designing a build pipeline in Azure Pipelines. The pipeline requires a self-hosted agent. The build pipeline will run once daily and will take 30 minutes to complete. You need to recommend a compute type for the agent. The solution must minimize costs. What should you recommend?
options: A. an Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) cluster
C. an Azure virtual machine scale set
questions.explanation: If your pipelines are in Azure Pipelines, then you've got a convenient option to run your jobs using a Microsoft-hosted agent. With Microsoft- hosted agents, maintenance and upgrades are taken care of for you. Each time you run a pipeline, you get a fresh virtual machine. The virtual machine is discarded after one use. Microsoft-hosted agents can run jobs directly on the VM or in a container. Note: You can try a Microsoft-hosted agent for no charge.

questions.topic: Topic 8 - You have a project in Azure DevOps. You have an Azure Resource Group deployment project in Microsoft Visual Studio that is checked in to the
questions.questionText: You have a project in Azure DevOps. You have an Azure Resource Group deployment project in Microsoft Visual Studio that is checked in to the Azure DevOps project. You need to create a release pipeline that will deploy resources by using Azure Resource Manager templates. The solution must minimize administrative effort. Which task type should you include in the solution?
options: B. Azure RM Web App Deployment
questions.explanation: There are two different ways to deploy templates to Azure DevOps Services. Both methods provide the same results, so choose the one that best fits your workflow. 1. Add a single step to your build pipeline that runs the PowerShell script that's included in the Azure Resource Group deployment project (Deploy- AzureResourceGroup.ps1). The script copies artifacts and then deploys the template. 2. Add multiple Azure DevOps Services build steps, each one performing a stage task. The first option has the...

questions.topic: Topic 8 - You have an Azure DevOps project that contains a release pipeline and a Git repository.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure DevOps project that contains a release pipeline and a Git repository. When a new code revision is committed to the repository, a build and release is triggered. You need to ensure that release information for the pipeline is added automatically to the work items associated to the Git commit. What should you do?
options: A. Modify the Integrations options for the pipeline.
B. Modify the post-deployment conditions for the last stage of the pipeline.
C. Add an agentless job to the pipeline.
questions.explanation: To automatically add release information to work items associated with Git commits, you should modify the Integrations options for the pipeline. In the release pipeline Options > Integrations, you enable 'Report deployment status to the repository host' and 'Report deployment status to Work' settings. Incorrect answers: B (post-deployment conditions) is wrong because post-deployment conditions control gates and approvals, not work item updates. C (agentless job) is wrong because agentless jobs run server-side tasks but are not the mechanism for automatic work item updates.

questions.topic: Topic 8 - You plan to use Terraform to deploy an Azure resource group from a Windows system.
questions.questionText: You plan to use Terraform to deploy an Azure resource group from a Windows system. You need to install the required frameworks to support the planned deployment. questions.explanation: You can use the combination of Terraform and Yeoman. Terraform is a tool for creating infrastructure on Azure. Yeoman makes it easy to create Terraform modules. Terratest provides a collection of helper functions and patterns for common infrastructure testing tasks, like making HTTP requests and using SSH to access a speciffc virtual machine. questions.questionText: You have a free tier of an Azure DevOps organization named Contoso. Contoso contains 10 private projects. Each project has multiple jobs with no dependencies. You frequently run the jobs on five self-hosted agents but experience long build times and frequently queued builds. You need to minimize the number of queued builds and the time it takes to run the builds. What should you do?
options: A. Configure the pipelines to use the Microsoft-hosted agents.
questions.explanation: If you want Azure Pipelines to orchestrate your builds and releases, but use your own machines to run them, use self-hosted parallel jobs. For self-hosted parallel jobs, you'll start by deploying our self-hosted agents on your machines. You can register any number of these self-hosted agents in your organization. Incorrect: Not D: Microsoft-hosted CI/CD - If you want to run your jobs on machines that Microsoft manages, use Microsoft-hosted parallel jobs. Your jobs run on our pool of Microsoft- h...

questions.questionText: You have an Azure DevOps project named Project1 and an Azure subscription named Sub1. Sub1 contains an Azure SQL database named DB1. You need to create a release pipeline that uses the Azure SQL Database Deployment task to update DB1. Which artifact should you deploy?
questions.explanation: Use Azure SQL Database Deployment task in a build or release pipeline to deploy to Azure SQL DB using a DACPAC or run scripts using SQLCMD.

questions.topic: Topic 8 - Your company has a project in Azure DevOps for a new web application.
questions.questionText: Your company has a project in Azure DevOps for a new web application. The company uses ServiceNow for change management. You need to ensure that a change request is processed before any components can be deployed to the production environment. What are two ways to integrate ServiceNow into the Azure DevOps release pipeline? Each correct answer presents a complete solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
options: A. Define a deployment control that invokes the ServiceNow REST API.
B. Define a pre-deployment gate before the deployment to the Prod stage.
C. Define a deployment control that invokes the ServiceNow SOAP API.
questions.explanation: Both options integrate ServiceNow into the release pipeline: A) Define a deployment control that invokes the ServiceNow REST API to check change request status before deployment. B) Define a pre-deployment gate before the Prod stage that uses the ServiceNow Change Management extension to validate that a change request is approved. Both approaches ensure a change request is processed before production deployment. Incorrect answer: C (ServiceNow SOAP API) is wrong because the Azure DevOps ServiceNow integration uses REST APIs, not SOAP APIs.

questions.topic: Topic 8 - Your company develops an application named App1 that is deployed in production.
questions.questionText: Your company develops an application named App1 that is deployed in production. As part of an application update, a new service is being added to App1. The new service requires access to an application named App2 that is currently in development. You need to ensure that you can deploy the update to App1 before App2 becomes available. You must be able to enable the service in App1 once App2 is deployed. What should you do?
options: B. Create a fork in the build.
C. Create a branch in the build.

questions.questionText: You are developing an iOS application by using Azure DevOps. You need to test the application manually on 10 devices without releasing the application to the public.
