questions.topic: Topic 8 - Your company has an on-premises Bitbucket Server that is used for Git-based source control. The server is protected by a firewall that blocks
questions.questionText: Your company has an on-premises Bitbucket Server that is used for Git-based source control. The server is protected by a firewall that blocks inbound Internet trafic. You plan to use Azure DevOps to manage the build and release processes. Which two components are required to integrate Azure DevOps and Bitbucket? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
questions.explanation: C: Service hooks are needed to notify Azure DevOps when code changes occur in the on-premises Bitbucket Server. D: A self-hosted agent is required because the Bitbucket Server is behind a firewall that blocks inbound Internet traffic, so a Microsoft-hosted agent cannot reach it. The self-hosted agent runs within the corporate network and can access the Bitbucket Server. Incorrect answers: A (deployment group) is wrong because deployment groups are for deploying to target machines, not for source control integration. B (Microsoft-hosted agent) is wrong because Microsoft-hosted agents run in Azure and cannot access the on-premises Bitbucket Server behind a firewall.

questions.topic: Topic 8 - You have a project in Azure DevOps.
questions.questionText: You have a project in Azure DevOps. You plan to deploy a self-hosted agent by using an unattended configuration script. Which two values should you define in the configuration script? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
questions.explanation: Unattended config: The agent can be set up from a script with no human intervention. You must pass --unattended and the answers to all questions. To configure an agent, it must know the URL to your organization or collection and credentials of someone authorized to set up agents. All other responses are optional.

questions.explanation: Batching CI runs - If you have many team members uploading changes often, you may want to reduce the number of runs you start. If you set batch to true, when a pipeline is running, the system waits until the run is completed, then starts another run with all changes that have not yet been built. Example: # speciffc branch build with batching trigger: batch: true branches: include: - master To clarify this example, let us say that a push A to master caused the above pipeline to run. While that pip...

questions.topic: Topic 8 - You are using GitHub as a source code repository.
questions.questionText: You are using GitHub as a source code repository. You create a client-side Git hook on the commit-msg event. The hook requires that each commit message contain a custom work item tag. You need to make a commit that does not have a work item tag. Which git commit parameter should you use?
questions.explanation: The commit-msg hook is invoked by git-commit and git-merge, and can be bypassed with the --no-verify option.

questions.topic: Topic 8 - You have a GitHub repository that contains workflows. The workflows contain steps that execute predefined actions. Each action has one or more
questions.questionText: You have a GitHub repository that contains workflows. The workflows contain steps that execute predefined actions. Each action has one or more versions. You need to request the speciffc version of an action to execute. Which three attributes can you use to identify the version? Each correct answer presents a complete solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
questions.explanation: In GitHub Actions, you can reference a specific version of an action using three attributes: A) SHA-based hashes (commit SHAs) for the most precise version pinning, B) tags (like v1.0.0) for semantic version references, and D) branches (like main or releases/v1) for tracking the latest code on a branch. Incorrect answer: C (runner) is wrong because the runner is the execution environment for the workflow, not an attribute for identifying action versions.

questions.topic: Topic 8 - You have an Azure subscription that contains multiple Azure pipelines.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains multiple Azure pipelines. You need to deploy a monitoring solution for the pipelines. The solution must meet the following requirements: ✑ Parse logs from multiple sources. ✑ Identify the root cause of issues. What advanced feature of a monitoring tool should you include in the solution?
questions.explanation: Analytics is the advanced monitoring feature that can parse logs from multiple sources and identify the root cause of issues. Analytics tools use machine learning and pattern recognition to correlate events across multiple data sources, helping to determine the underlying cause of pipeline failures. Incorrect answers: B (synthetic monitoring) is wrong because it simulates user interactions to test availability, not analyze logs. C (directed monitoring) is wrong because it focuses on monitoring specific known metrics, not root cause analysis across multiple sources.

questions.topic: Topic 8 - You use GitHub for source control of .NET applications.
questions.questionText: You use GitHub for source control of .NET applications. You need to deploy a documentation solution that meets the following requirements: ✑ Documents will be written in Markdown as developers make code changes. ✑ Changes to the documents will trigger the recompilation of a static website. ✑ Users will access the documents from the static website. ✑ Documents will be stored in a GitHub repository. Which two tools can you use to compile the website? Each correct answer presents a complete solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
questions.explanation: DocFX and Jekyll are both tools that can generate documentation websites from source code repositories. DocFX is specifically designed for .NET documentation and can generate API reference documentation from source code comments. Jekyll is a static site generator commonly used with GitHub Pages. Incorrect answers: A (WordPress) is wrong because it is a content management system, not a documentation generation tool for source code. D (caret) is wrong because it is not a documentation generation tool.

questions.topic: Topic 8 - You have an on-premises app named App1 that accesses Azure resources by using credentials stored in a configuration file.
questions.questionText: You have an on-premises app named App1 that accesses Azure resources by using credentials stored in a configuration file. You plan to upgrade App1 to use an Azure service principal. What is required for App1 to programmatically sign in to Azure Active Directory (Azure AD)?
options: A. the application ID, a client secret, and the object ID
B. a client secret, the object ID, and the tenant ID
C. the application ID, a client secret, and the tenant ID
questions.explanation: To programmatically sign in to Azure AD using a service principal, you need three values: the application (client) ID, a client secret (or certificate), and the tenant ID. The application ID identifies the service principal, the client secret authenticates it, and the tenant ID specifies which Azure AD directory to authenticate against. Incorrect answers: A (application ID, client secret, object ID) is wrong because the object ID is not used for programmatic sign-in. B (client secret, object ID, tenant ID) is wrong because the object ID is not needed and the application ID is missing.

questions.topic: Topic 8 - You have an Azure subscription that contains 50 virtual machines.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains 50 virtual machines. You plan to manage the configuration of the virtual machines by using Azure Automation State Configuration. You need to create the Desired State Configuration (DSC) configuration files. How should you structure the code blocks?
questions.explanation: In PowerShell DSC, the code structure follows: Configuration (outer block that defines the configuration) > Node (specifies target machines) > Resource (defines the desired state of specific components). The Configuration block is the outermost container, Node blocks define which machines to configure, and Resource blocks specify what to configure. Incorrect answers: B and C are wrong because they do not follow the correct DSC hierarchy of Configuration > Node > Resource.

questions.topic: Topic 8 - You have a project in Azure DevOps named Project1 that contains two environments named environment1 and environment2.
questions.questionText: You have a project in Azure DevOps named Project1 that contains two environments named environment1 and environment2. When a new version of Project is released, the latest version is deployed to environment2, and the previous version is redeployed to environment1. You need to distribute users across the environments. The solution must meet the following requirements: • New releases must be available to only a subset of the users. • You must gradually increase the number of users that can access environment2. What should you use?
questions.explanation: Azure App Service deployment slots provide a zero-downtime deployment mechanism with instant rollback capability. You deploy to a staging slot, verify the deployment, then swap the staging slot with the production slot. Rollback is simply swapping back. Incorrect answers: A (VIP swapping) is wrong because VIP swap is a legacy Azure Cloud Services concept. C (Azure Load Balancer) is wrong because while it can distribute traffic, it does not provide the built-in swap and rollback functionality of deployment slots.

questions.topic: Topic 8 - You have a project in Azure DevOps named Project1.
questions.questionText: You have a project in Azure DevOps named Project1. You need to ensure that all new pipelines in Project1 execute three speciffc tasks during pipeline execution. What should you create?
questions.explanation: A task group encapsulates a sequence of tasks that can be reused across multiple build and release pipelines. By creating a task group with the three specific tasks, you ensure all new pipelines in Project1 include those tasks. Incorrect answers: B (JSON template) is wrong because Azure Pipelines does not use JSON templates for defining reusable task sequences. C (YAML template) is wrong because while YAML templates can define reusable steps, the question specifies task groups which are the classic pipeline approach for ensuring specific tasks are included.

questions.topic: Topic 8 - You use Azure DevOps processes to build and deploy code.
questions.questionText: You use Azure DevOps processes to build and deploy code. You need to compare how much time is spent troubleshooting issues found during development and how much time is spent troubleshooting issues found in released code. Which KPI should you use?
questions.explanation: The defect escape rate measures the ratio of defects found in production versus defects found during development. It compares time spent troubleshooting development-phase issues against time spent on released-code issues. A lower escape rate indicates better quality gates in the development process. Incorrect answers: B (unplanned work rate) is wrong because it measures the ratio of unplanned to planned work, not defect distribution. C (defect rate) is wrong because it measures the total number of defects, not the comparison between development and production defects.

questions.topic: Topic 8 - You have a project in Azure DevOps named Project1.
questions.questionText: You have a project in Azure DevOps named Project1. You implement a Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) pipeline that uses PowerShell Desired State Configuration (DSC) to configure the application infrastructure. You need to perform a unit test and an integration test of the configuration before Project1 is deployed. What should you use?
questions.explanation: The Pester test framework is the standard testing framework for PowerShell, including PowerShell DSC configurations. Pester can perform both unit tests (testing individual DSC resources) and integration tests (testing the complete configuration) before deployment. Incorrect answers: A (PSScriptAnalyzer) is wrong because it performs static code analysis on PowerShell scripts for style and best practices, not unit or integration testing of DSC configurations. C (PSCodeHealth) is wrong because it generates code health reports but does not perform unit or integration testing of DSC configurations.

questions.topic: Topic 9 - You plan to create an image that will contain a .NET Core application.
questions.questionText: You plan to create an image that will contain a .NET Core application. You have a Dockerfile file that contains the following code. (Line numbers are included for reference only.) You need to ensure that the image is as small as possible when the image is built. Which line should you modify in the file?
questions.explanation: To minimize Docker image size, you should modify the FROM instruction (line 1 in a typical Dockerfile) to use a smaller base image, such as an Alpine-based or runtime-only image. Using multi-stage builds with a smaller final base image is the most effective way to reduce image size. Incorrect answers: B (line 3) and C (line 4) are wrong because changing COPY or RUN instructions has less impact on final image size compared to choosing a smaller base image.

questions.topic: Topic 9 - You have an Azure web app that is deployed by using Azure Pipelines.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure web app that is deployed by using Azure Pipelines. You need to ensure that when a new version of the app is deployed to production, you can roll back to the previous version. The solution must meet the following requirements: • Minimize downtime during the deployment. • Minimize the time it takes for the rollback. What should you use?
options: A. a single web app and two deployment slots
B. a single web app and two deployment pipelines
C. two web apps and an Azure Standard Load Balancer
questions.explanation: Using a single web app with two deployment slots provides the fastest rollback with minimal downtime. You deploy the new version to the staging slot, and when ready, swap it with the production slot. If issues arise, you simply swap back to the previous slot, which still contains the old version. This process takes only seconds. Incorrect answers: B (two deployment pipelines) is wrong because having two pipelines does not inherently provide fast rollback. C (two web apps with Load Balancer) is wrong because switching traffic between two separate web apps is more complex and slower than slot swapping.

questions.topic: Topic 9 - You are automating the testing process for your company.
questions.questionText: You are automating the testing process for your company. You need to automate UI testing of a web application. Which framework should you use?
questions.explanation: Playwright is a modern UI testing framework that supports cross-browser automation for web applications. It is developed by Microsoft and integrates well with Azure DevOps pipelines. Playwright supports Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit browsers and is designed for reliable end-to-end testing. Incorrect answers: A (JaCoCo) is wrong because JaCoCo is a code coverage tool for Java, not a UI testing framework. C (Xamarin.UITest) is wrong because it is designed for testing mobile applications (iOS and Android), not web applications.

questions.topic: Topic 9 - You have a project in Azure DevOps named Project1 that references an Azure Artifacts feed named Feed1.
questions.questionText: You have a project in Azure DevOps named Project1 that references an Azure Artifacts feed named Feed1. You have a package named Package1 that has the versions shown in the following table. You need to perform a build of Project1. Which version of Package1 will be used?
questions.explanation: In Azure Artifacts, the package version used in a build depends on the feed view configuration and version resolution rules. The highest stable version available in the configured feed view will typically be used. Please refer to Azure Artifacts documentation on package versioning and feed views for the specific version resolution logic applicable to your configuration.

Overview - General Overview - Woodgrove Bank is a financial services company that has a main offce in the United Kingdom. Technical Requirements and Planned Changes Planned Changes - Woodgrove Bank plans to implement the following project management changes: Implement Azure DevOps for project tracking. Centralize source code control in private GitHub repositories. Implement Azure Pipelines for build pipelines and release pipelines. Woodgrove Bank plans to implement the following changes to the identity environment: Deploy an Azure AD tenant named woodgrovebank.com. Sync the Active Directory domain to Azure AD. Configure App1 to use a service principal. Integrate GitHub with Azure AD. Woodgrove Bank plans to implement the following changes to the core apps: Migrate App1 to ASP.NET Core. Integrate Azure Pipelines and the third-party build tool used to develop App2. Woodgrove Bank plans to implement the following changes to the DevOps environment: Deploy App1 to Azure App Service. Implement source control for the DB1 schema. Migrate all the source code from TFS1 to GitHub. Deploy App2 to an Azure virtual machine named VM1. Merge the POC branch into the GitHub default branch. Implement an Azure DevOps dashboard for stakeholders to monitor development progress. Technical Requirements - Woodgrove Bank identiffes the following technical requirements: The initial databases for new environments must contain both schema and reference data. An Azure Monitor alert for VM1 must be configured to meet the following requirements: - Be triggered when average CPU usage exceeds 80 percent for 15 minutes. - Calculate CPU usage averages once every minute. The commit history of the POC branch must replace the history of the default branch. The Azure DevOps dashboard must display the metrics shown in the following table. https://www.examtopics.com/exams/microsoft/az-400/custom-view/ 368/404 7/10/23, 7:53 PM AZ-400 Exam – Free Actual Q&As, Page 1 | ExamTopics Access to Azure DevOps must be restricted to speciffc IP addresses. Page load times for App1 must be captured and monitored. Administrative effort must be minimized. Question You need to the merge the POC branch into the default branch. The solution must meet the technical requirements. Which command should you run?
questions.explanation: The commit history of the POC branch must replace the history of the default branch. Rebasing is the process of moving or combining a sequence of commits to a new base commit. Rebasing is most useful and easily visualized in the context of a feature branching workflow. Existing Environment - Contoso plans to improve its IT development and operations processes by implementing Azure DevOps principles. Contoso has an Azure subscription and creates an Azure DevOps organization. The Azure DevOps organization includes: The Docker extension A deployment pool named Pool7 that contains 10 Azure virtual machines that run Windows Server 2019 The Azure subscription contains an Azure Automation account. Requirements - Planned changes - Contoso plans to create projects in Azure DevOps as shown in the following table. Technical requirements - Contoso identiffes the following technical requirements: Implement build agents for Project1. Whenever possible, use Azure resources. Avoid using deprecated technologies. Implement a code flow strategy for Project2 that will: - Enable Team2 to submit pull requests for Project2. - Enable Team2 to work independently on changes to a copy of Project2. https://www.examtopics.com/exams/microsoft/az-400/custom-view/ 376/404 7/10/23, 7:53 PM AZ-400 Exam – Free Actual Q&As, Page 1 | ExamTopics - Ensure that any intermediary changes performed by Team2 on a copy of Project2 will be subject to the same restrictions as the ones defined in the build policy of Project2. Whenever possible, implement automation and minimize administrative effort. Implement Project3, Project5, Project6, and Project7 based on the planned changes. Implement Project4 and configure the project to push Docker images to Azure Container Registry. Question In Azure DevOps, you create Project3. You need to meet the requirements of the project. What should you do first?
options: A. From Azure DevOps, modify the build definition.
B. From SonarQube, obtain an authentication token.
C. From Azure DevOps, create a service endpoint.
questions.explanation: The first thing to do is to declare your SonarQube server as a service endpoint in your VSTS/DevOps project settings.

options: A. Add the FROM instruction in the Dockerfile file.
B. Add a Copy and Publish Build Artifacts task to the build pipeline.

questions.explanation: Scenario: Implement Project4 and configure the project to push Docker images to Azure Container Registry. You use Azure Container Registry Tasks commands to quickly build, push, and run a Docker container image natively within Azure, showing how to offoad your "inner-loop" development cycle to the cloud. ACR Tasks is a suite of features within Azure Container Registry to help you manage and modify container images across the container lifecycle.

When you are ready to answer a question, click the Question button to return to the question. Overview - Litware, Inc. is an independent software vendor (ISV). Litware has a main offce and five branch offces. Existing Environment - Application Architecture - The company's primary application is a single monolithic retirement fund management system based on ASP.NET web forms that use logic written in VB.NET. Some new sections of the application are written in C#. Variations of the application are created for individual customers. Currently, there are more than 80 live code branches in the application's code base. The application was developed by using Microsoft Visual Studio. Source code is stored in Team Foundation Server (TFS) in the main offce. The branch offces access the source code by using TFS proxy servers. Architectural Issues - Litware focuses on writing new code for customers. No resources are provided to refactor or remove existing code. Changes to the code base take a long time, as dependencies are not obvious to individual developers. Merge operations of the code often take months and involve many developers. Code merging frequently introduces bugs that are diffcult to locate and resolve. Customers report that ownership costs of the retirement fund management system increase continually. The need to merge unrelated code makes even minor code changes expensive. Customers report that bug reporting is overly complex. Requirements - Planned Changes - Litware plans to develop a new suite of applications for investment planning. The investment planning applications will require only minor integration with the existing retirement fund management system. The investment planning applications suite will include one multi-tier web application and two iOS mobile applications. One mobile application will be used by employees; the other will be used by customers. Litware plans to move to a more agile development methodology. Shared code will be extracted into a series of packages. Litware has started an internal cloud transformation process and plans to use cloud-based services whenever suitable. Litware wants to become proactive in detecting failures, rather than always waiting for customer bug reports. Technical Requirements - https://www.examtopics.com/exams/microsoft/az-400/custom-view/ 386/404 7/10/23, 7:53 PM AZ-400 Exam – Free Actual Q&As, Page 1 | ExamTopics The company's investment planning applications suite must meet the following technical requirements: New incoming connections through the firewall must be minimized. Members of a group named Developers must be able to install packages. The principle of least privilege must be used for all permission assignments. A branching strategy that supports developing new functionality in isolation must be used. Members of a group named Team Leaders must be able to create new packages and edit the permissions of package feeds. Visual Studio App Center must be used to centralize the reporting of mobile application crashes and device types in use. By default, all releases must remain available for 30 days, except for production releases, which must be kept for 60 days. Code quality and release quality are critical. During release, deployments must not proceed between stages if any active bugs are logged against the release. The mobile applications must be able to call the share pricing service of the existing retirement fund management system. Until the system is upgraded, the service will only support basic authentication over HTTPS. The required operating system configuration for the test servers changes weekly. Azure Automation State Configuration must be used to ensure that the operating system on each test server is configured the same way when the servers are created and checked periodically. Current Technical Issue - The test servers are configured correctly when first deployed, but they experience configuration drift over time. Azure Automation State Configuration fails to correct the configurations. Azure Automation State Configuration nodes are registered by using the following command. Question To resolve the current technical issue, what should you do to the Register-AzureRmAutomationDscNode command?
options: A. Change the value of the ConfigurationMode parameter.
B. Replace the Register-AzureRmAutomationDscNode cmdlet with Register-AzureRmAutomationScheduledRunbook
questions.explanation: Change the ConfigurationMode parameter from ApplyOnly to ApplyAndAutocorrect. The Register-AzureRmAutomationDscNode cmdlet registers an Azure virtual machine as an APS Desired State Configuration (DSC) node in an Azure Automation account. Scenario: Current Technical Issue The test servers are configured correctly when first deployed, but they experience configuration drift over time. Azure Automation State Configuration fails to correct the configurations. Azure Automation State Configuration nodes are ...

Question What should you use to implement the code quality restriction on the release pipeline for the investment planning applications suite?
questions.explanation: When a release is created from a release pipeline that defines approvals, the deployment stops at each point where approval is required until the speciffed approver grants approval or rejects the release (or re-assigns the approval to another user). Scenario: Code quality and release quality are critical. During release, deployments must not proceed between stages if any active bugs are logged against the release.

The solution must meet the technical requirements. You need to prepare the database for the deployment. How should you format the export?
questions.explanation: The initial databases for new environments must contain both schema and reference data. When you need to export a database for archiving or for moving to another platform, you can export the database schema and data to a BACPAC file. A BACPAC file is a ZIP file with an extension of BACPAC containing the metadata and data from the database. A BACPAC file can be stored in Azure Blob storage or in local storage in an on-premises location and later imported back into Azure SQL Database, Azure SQL Manage...

Page load times for App1 must be captured and monitored. Administrative effort must be minimized. Question You need to meet the technical requirements for monitoring App1. What should you use?
questions.explanation: Azure Application Insights should be used to capture and monitor page load times for App1. Application Insights provides detailed performance monitoring including page load times, server response times, and dependency tracking. It integrates natively with Azure App Service. Scenario: Page load times for App1 must be captured and monitored. Incorrect answers: A (Splunk) is wrong because while Splunk can monitor applications, it is not an Azure-native solution and would require more administrative effort. The other option is wrong because Azure Monitor alone does not provide the detailed page load time metrics that Application Insights offers.
